cves.name: Log4Shell
cves.description: Found in December 2021 in Java Log4j library. Entire internet was affected — Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, Tesla.
cves.how_exploit: Attacker sends this string in any input. Log4j executes it while logging — downloads and runs code from attacker server. Full Remote Code Execution.
cves.fix: Upgrade to Log4j 2.17.1+. Disable JNDI lookups.

cves.name: Heartbleed
cves.description: Memory leak in OpenSSL library. Attackers could read server private memory — passwords, private keys, session tokens.
cves.vulnerable_code:
memcpy(bp, p, payload);
// payload size never validated!
cves.how_exploit: Attacker sends heartbeat request with larger payload size than actual data. Server returns extra memory without validation — sensitive data leaked.
cves.fix: Update OpenSSL to version 1.0.1g or higher.
cves.fixed_code:
if (payload + padding > s->s3->rrec.length)
    return 0; // Reject invalid request

cves.name: SQL Injection Classic
cves.description: Most common and dangerous vulnerability. Allows attackers to directly control the database.
cves.vulnerable_code: query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name='" + username + "'"
cves.how_exploit:
Enter as username: ' OR '1'='1
Query becomes: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name='' OR '1'='1'
Result: Entire database exposed!
cves.fix: Use parameterized queries — keep user input separate from SQL query.

cves.name: IDOR - Broken Access Control
cves.description: OWASP #1 vulnerability in 2021. Changing the ID in a URL allows access to another user's data.
cves.vulnerable_code:
# URL: /profile?id=123
user = db.get_user(request.args.get('id'))
return user.data  # No authorization check!
cves.how_exploit: Change id=123 to id=124 in URL — another user's profile is returned. No server-side authorization check exists.
cves.fix: Always perform server-side authorization checks on every request.
cves.fixed_code:
user = db.get_user(request.args.get('id'))
if user.id != current_user.id:
    return 403
return user.data

cves.name: Broken Authentication
cves.description: Weak authentication that allows attackers to hijack user accounts easily.
cves.how_exploit: Weak JWT secrets can be brute forced. Plain text passwords are immediately exposed in database leaks.
cves.fix: Use strong secrets, hash all passwords, implement MFA.
cves.fixed_code:
import secrets, bcrypt
jwt_secret = secrets.token_hex(32)
hashed = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt())
